places.note_ko: 완전하게 지어진 세상에 죄가 들어와, 하나님과 사람 사이가 끊어진다.
places.note_en: Into a world made very good, sin enters and breaks the bond between God and humanity.
events_ko: 첫 복음의 약속 — “여자의 후손이 뱀의 머리를 상하게 하리라”
events_en: Creation in six days — “it was very good”
Humanity made in the image of God
The fall, and exile from Eden
The first gospel promise — “the offspring of the woman will crush the serpent’s head”

places.note_ko: 죄로 가득한 세상을 심판하시되, 한 가정을 통해 생명을 보존하신다.
places.note_en: God judges a world full of sin, yet preserves life through one family.
events_en: A world filled with corruption
The flood, and Noah’s ark
The rainbow covenant — “never again a flood to destroy”

places.note_ko: 하늘에 닿으려는 교만에, 언어를 흩으시고 사람들을 온 땅에 흩으신다.
places.note_en: Against the pride that reaches for heaven, God scatters their speech and the peoples over the earth.
events_ko: 사람들이 온 땅으로 흩어짐 (훗날의 바벨론, 시날 평지)
events_en: “Let us build a city and a tower… and make a name for ourselves”
God confuses their language
The peoples scatter over the earth (Shinar — later Babylon)

places.note_ko: 성경의 구원 이야기가 한 사람의 부르심으로 시작된다.
places.note_en: The Bible’s story of redemption begins with the call of one man.
events_ko: “큰 민족을 이루고 땅의 모든 족속이 너로 복을 얻으리라”
events_en: God calls Abram out of Ur of the Chaldeans
“I will make of you a great nation… all peoples blessed through you”
By faith he leaves his homeland

places.note_ko: 부르심에 응답해 약속의 땅을 향해 출발하는 길목.
places.note_en: On the way, answering the call toward the promised land.
events_en: Terah’s family settles in Haran
Terah dies
Abram sets out for Canaan at age 75

places.note_ko: 약속의 땅에서 한 가족이 세워지고, 언약이 대를 잇는다.
places.note_en: In the promised land a family is founded and the covenant passes down the generations.
events_ko: 가나안 입성, 첫 제단 — “이 땅을 네 자손에게 주리라”
야곱의 열두 아들 = 열두 지파의 뿌리
events_en: Entering Canaan, first altar — “to your offspring I give this land”
The covenant (Gen 15) · Isaac · Jacob
Jacob’s twelve sons — root of the twelve tribes

places.note_ko: 한 가족이 큰 민족이 되지만, 이내 노예가 된다.
places.note_en: A family becomes a great nation — then a nation of slaves.
events_ko: 기근을 피해 야곱의 온 가족이 내려옴
events_en: Joseph, sold away, rises to rule Egypt
Jacob’s whole family comes down to escape famine
The growing nation of Israel is enslaved

places.note_ko: 구약의 핵심 구원 사건 — 노예가 하나님의 백성이 되다.
places.note_en: The OT’s central rescue — slaves become God’s own people.
events_ko: 열 재앙과 유월절 어린 양의 피
events_en: The ten plagues and the Passover lamb
The Red Sea parts and they cross
Ten Commandments, the Law, the tabernacle and sacrifices

places.note_ko: 불신으로 한 세대가 광야를 떠돌지만, 하나님은 함께하신다.
places.note_en: Unbelief costs a generation the land — yet God stays with them.
events_en: Twelve spies — but the people refuse to trust
38 years in the desert — manna, cloud and fire
On the plains of Moab, Moses’ last sermon (Deuteronomy)

places.note_ko: 마침내 약속의 땅에 들어가 정착한다.
places.note_en: At last they enter and take the promised land.
events_ko: 가나안 정복과 열두 지파의 땅 분배
events_en: The Jordan parts and the people cross
The walls of Jericho fall
Conquest of Canaan and division of the land

places.note_ko: 왕이 없어 제멋대로 행하던 시대 — 참된 왕이 필요하다.
places.note_en: With no king, everyone does as he pleases — a true king is needed.
events_ko: 룻 — 어두운 시대의 신실함, 다윗의 족보
events_en: The tabernacle is set up at Shiloh
The cycle of sin–oppression–rescue (Deborah, Gideon, Samson)
Ruth — faithfulness in dark days, in David’s line

places.note_ko: 이스라엘의 황금기, 그리고 영원한 왕에 대한 약속.
places.note_en: Israel’s golden age — and the promise of an everlasting king.
events_ko: 첫 왕 사울, 그리고 다윗과 골리앗
events_en: Saul the first king, then David and Goliath
The Davidic covenant — “your throne forever” (2 Sam 7)
Solomon builds the temple in Jerusalem

places.note_ko: 나라가 둘로 갈라져 점점 타락하고, 선지자들이 외친다.
places.note_en: The kingdom splits and declines as the prophets cry out.
events_en: After Solomon, the split into Judah and Israel
Repeated idolatry
Elijah, Isaiah, Jeremiah cry ‘return’ — and foretell the Messiah

places.note_ko: 경고를 무시한 북이스라엘이 먼저 무너진다.
places.note_en: Ignoring every warning, the north falls first.
events_en: Nineveh, the capital of Assyria
The northern kingdom falls to Assyria (722 BC)
Jonah is sent to Nineveh

places.note_ko: 가장 어두운 밤 — 그러나 회복의 약속은 남는다.
places.note_en: The darkest night — yet the promise of restoration remains.
events_en: Jerusalem falls, the temple is burned (586 BC)
Judah is carried off to Babylon
Daniel and Ezekiel — faith and visions in exile

places.note_ko: 이방 제국 한복판에서도 하나님이 백성을 지키신다.
places.note_en: Even at the heart of a pagan empire, God keeps his people.
events_en: Persia overthrows Babylon
Esther saves the Jews from destruction
Nehemiah prays before the king and is sent

places.note_ko: 돌아와 다시 세우지만 — 왕은 없다. 메시아를 기다린다.
places.note_en: They return and rebuild — but no king has come. They await Messiah.
events_ko: 말라기 — “내가 내 사자를 보내리라”로 구약이 닫힘
events_en: Three waves of return by Cyrus’ decree (Zerubbabel 538 · Ezra 458 · Nehemiah 444)
Temple rebuilt and Jerusalem’s walls restored
Malachi — the OT closes: “I will send my messenger”

places.note_ko: 약속하신 메시아가 다윗의 동네에 오신다 — 말씀이 육신이 되심.
places.note_en: The promised Messiah comes to the town of David — the Word made flesh.
events_en: Born in Bethlehem, the town of David
Angels announce the news to shepherds
The Magi come to worship

places.note_ko: 하나님이 평범한 마을에서 사람으로 자라신다.
places.note_en: God grows up as a man in an ordinary town.
events_en: Raised in a carpenter’s home
At twelve, asking and answering in the temple
Later rejected in his hometown synagogue

places.note_ko: 공생애가 시작된다 — 하늘이 그를 아들로 인 치신다.
places.note_en: His public ministry begins — heaven seals him as the Son.
events_ko: 성령이 비둘기같이 임함 — “이는 내 사랑하는 아들”
events_en: Baptized by John
The Spirit descends like a dove — “This is my beloved Son”
Overcomes Satan’s three temptations in the wilderness

places.note_ko: 첫 표적으로 자기 영광을 나타내신다.
places.note_en: In his first sign he reveals his glory.
events_ko: 물을 포도주로 — 예수님의 첫 번째 표적
events_en: A guest at a wedding
Water turned to wine — his first sign
His disciples believe in him

places.note_ko: 초기 사역의 길 — 예루살렘을 거쳐 사마리아에까지 복음이 닿는다.
places.note_en: The early ministry road — through Jerusalem and on to despised Samaria.
events_en: He cleanses the temple in Jerusalem
By night, Nicodemus — “You must be born again”
Living water for a Samaritan woman

places.note_ko: 갈릴리 사역의 본거지 — 부르심·가르침·반대가 한데 모인다.
places.note_en: Home base of the Galilean ministry — calling, teaching and opposition all meet here.
events_ko: 천국의 비유와, 커져 가는 바리새인의 반대
events_en: Calls the fishermen and bases himself here
The Sermon on the Mount — “Blessed are the poor in spirit”
Parables of the kingdom — and rising opposition

places.note_ko: 자연을 다스리는 권세로 자신이 누구신지 보이신다.
places.note_en: With power over nature he shows who he is.
events_en: “Peace, be still” — he calms the storm
Feeds 5,000 with five loaves and two fish
Walks on the water

places.note_ko: 제자들이 그가 그리스도이심을 깨닫고, 십자가의 길이 드러난다.
places.note_en: The disciples grasp that he is the Christ, and the way of the cross opens.
events_ko: 베드로의 고백 — “주는 그리스도시요 살아 계신 하나님의 아들”
events_en: Peter’s confession — “You are the Christ, the Son of the living God”
He first foretells his death and resurrection
Transfigured in glory on the mountain

places.note_ko: 예루살렘으로 올라가는 길, 잃은 자를 찾으신다.
places.note_en: On the road up to Jerusalem, he seeks the lost.
events_ko: “인자가 온 것은 잃어버린 자를 찾아 구원하려 함이라”
events_en: He gives sight to blind Bartimaeus
He calls Zacchaeus the tax collector
“The Son of Man came to seek and save the lost”

places.note_ko: 죽음을 이기는 분임을 보이시며, 마지막 한 주를 앞두신다.
places.note_en: He shows mastery over death on the eve of his final week.
events_ko: 죽은 나사로를 무덤에서 살리심 — “나는 부활이요 생명이니”
events_en: He raises Lazarus from the tomb — “I am the resurrection and the life”
Mary anoints him for burial
This sign hardens the plot to kill him

places.note_ko: 성경 전체가 향해 온 절정 — 십자가·부활·승천.
places.note_en: The climax the whole Bible points to — cross, resurrection, ascension.
events_en: Triumphal entry; he overturns the temple tables again
At the Last Supper he washes the disciples’ feet
He dies on the cross
Risen on the third day, then ascended

places.note_ko: 이방 선교가 시작되고 돌아오는 모(母) 교회.
places.note_en: The sending church where the Gentile mission begins and returns.
events_ko: 금식·기도 중 성령이 바나바와 바울을 따로 세움
events_en: In prayer the Spirit sets apart Barnabas and Saul
The church where believers were first called ‘Christians’
Launch point of the 1st, 2nd and 3rd journeys

places.note_ko: 안디옥의 항구 — 배를 타고 구브로로 떠난다.
places.note_en: Antioch’s port — they set sail for Cyprus.

places.note_ko: 구브로 섬에 첫발 — 회당에서 말씀을 전한다.
places.note_en: First stop on Cyprus — preaching in the synagogues.

places.note_ko: 복음을 막는 세력 앞에서 권능이 나타난다.
places.note_en: Power is shown against those who oppose the gospel.
events_en: Elymas the magician is struck blind
Sergius Paulus the proconsul believes
From here ‘Paul’ takes the lead

places.note_ko: 밤빌리아의 항구 — 여기서 마가 요한이 떠난다.
places.note_en: A port in Pamphylia — here John Mark turns back.

places.note_ko: 이방인을 향한 복음이 본격적으로 터져 나온다.
places.note_en: The gospel breaks open toward the Gentiles.
events_en: A long sermon of salvation history in the synagogue
Many Gentiles rejoice and receive the word
Driven out by opposition

places.note_ko: 큰 무리가 믿었으나 핍박을 피해 떠난다.
places.note_en: Many believe, but persecution forces them on.

places.note_ko: 신으로 떠받들렸다가, 곧 돌에 맞는다.
places.note_en: Hailed as a god, then stoned.
events_ko: 돌에 맞아 죽은 줄 알았으나 다시 일어남
events_en: Heals a man lame from birth
The crowd tries to sacrifice to them as gods
Stoned and left for dead, he gets up

places.note_ko: 많은 제자를 얻고, 왔던 길로 돌아가 교회를 굳게 한다.
places.note_en: Many disciples are made; they retrace their steps to strengthen the churches.

places.note_ko: 복음이 처음으로 유럽을 향하는 전환점.
places.note_en: The turning point where the gospel first heads for Europe.
events_en: A vision of a man of Macedonia
“Come over and help us”
They cross at once into Europe

places.note_ko: 유럽의 첫 교회 — 감옥에서도 복음이 이긴다.
places.note_en: Europe’s first church — the gospel triumphs even in prison.
events_ko: 한밤 찬송 중 지진, 간수의 온 집이 믿음
events_en: Lydia, a dealer in purple, believes
A fortune-telling slave girl is freed
Midnight hymns, an earthquake, the jailer’s household saved

places.note_ko: 말씀이 도시를 흔들고, 반대가 끓어오른다.
places.note_en: The word shakes the city and opposition boils over.
events_en: Reasons in the synagogue three Sabbaths
“These who have turned the world upside down”
They slip away by night

places.note_ko: 말씀을 스스로 살펴 받은 ‘신사적인’ 사람들.
places.note_en: The ‘noble’ ones who examined the word for themselves.
events_en: They receive the word eagerly
Daily they examine the Scriptures to check it
Many come to believe

places.note_ko: 철학의 도시에서 ‘알지 못하는 신’을 전한다.
places.note_en: In the city of philosophers he proclaims the ‘unknown god’.
events_en: Provoked by a city full of idols
On the Areopagus he preaches Creator and resurrection
Mocked, yet a few believe

places.note_ko: 환락의 도시에 1년 반을 머물며 교회를 세운다.
places.note_en: He stays a year and a half, planting a church in a city of vice.
events_en: Makes tents with Aquila and Priscilla
“Do not be afraid… for I am with you”
Many Corinthians believe and are baptized

places.note_ko: 3차 여행의 중심 — 아시아 전역에 말씀이 퍼진다.
places.note_en: Heart of the third journey — the word spreads across Asia.
events_en: Daily teaching for two years in the hall of Tyrannus
Magic books burned; powerful works
The silversmith Demetrius starts a riot

places.note_ko: 에베소 장로들과 눈물로 작별한다.
places.note_en: A tearful farewell to the Ephesian elders.
events_ko: 다시는 못 볼 줄 알고 함께 우는 장로들
events_en: “Care for the church he bought with his own blood”
The elders weep, knowing they’ll not see him again

places.note_ko: 총독·왕 앞에서 2년간 복음을 변론한다.
places.note_en: For two years he defends the gospel before governors and a king.
events_en: Trials before governors Felix and Festus
His defense before King Agrippa
“I appeal to Caesar” — on to Rome

places.note_ko: 성전에서 붙잡혀 죄수의 길이 시작된다.
places.note_en: Seized in the temple, his road as a prisoner begins.
events_en: Seized by the crowd in the temple
Defends himself before the council
As a Roman citizen, sent under guard to Caesarea

places.note_ko: 로마로 가는 알렉산드리아 배로 갈아탄다.
places.note_en: They transfer to an Alexandrian ship bound for Rome.

places.note_ko: 항해를 멈추라는 바울의 경고가 무시된다.
places.note_en: Paul’s warning to stop sailing goes unheeded.

places.note_ko: 석방 후, 디도를 남겨 교회를 세우게 한 섬.
places.note_en: After his release, the island where he leaves Titus to set the churches in order.
events_ko: 디도를 남겨 각 성에 장로를 세우게 함
events_en: Leaves Titus to appoint elders in every town
Calls for blameless, faithful leaders
Writes the letter to Titus

places.note_ko: 광풍과 난파 속에서도 하나님의 약속이 지켜진다.
places.note_en: Through storm and shipwreck, God’s promise holds.
events_ko: 유라굴로 광풍과 난파, 그러나 276명 전원 생존
events_en: The ‘northeaster’ wrecks the ship, yet all 276 survive
Bitten by a viper, he is unharmed
He heals the sick of the island

places.note_ko: 시칠리아에서 사흘을 머문다.
places.note_en: They stay three days in Sicily.

places.note_ko: 이탈리아에 상륙해 형제들을 만난다.
places.note_en: They land in Italy and are met by fellow believers.

places.note_ko: 복음이 마침내 제국의 심장에 닿는다 — 끝이 아니라 시작.
places.note_en: The gospel reaches the heart of the empire at last — not an end but a beginning.
events_ko: 셋집에서 2년간 머물며 거침없이 하나님의 나라를 전파
훗날 다시 갇혀 디모데후서를 쓰고 순교 — “내가 선한 싸움을 싸우고 나의 달려갈 길을 마치고”
events_en: Two years under house arrest, boldly proclaiming the kingdom
From here he writes the Prison Letters (Eph·Phil·Col·Phm)
Later imprisoned again, writes 2 Timothy and is martyred — “I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race”

journeys.note_ko: 안디옥에서 보냄받아 구브로와 소아시아 남부에 처음 복음을 전한다.
journeys.note_en: Sent from Antioch, the gospel reaches Cyprus and southern Asia Minor for the first time.

journeys.note_ko: 환상의 부름을 따라 바다를 건너 유럽(마케도니아·그리스)에 처음 복음이 들어간다.
journeys.note_en: Following a vision, Paul crosses into Europe — the gospel first enters Macedonia and Greece.

journeys.note_ko: 에베소에 오래 머물며 아시아 전역에 말씀이 퍼지고, 예루살렘으로 올라간다.
journeys.note_en: A long stay in Ephesus spreads the word across Asia; then Paul goes up to Jerusalem.

journeys.note_ko: 죄수가 되어, 풍랑과 난파를 지나 제국의 심장 로마까지 복음을 가지고 간다. (전통적으로 이 로마 항해를 넷째 여정으로 본다.)
journeys.note_en: As a prisoner, through storm and shipwreck, Paul carries the gospel to the heart of the empire — Rome. (This voyage is often counted as the fourth journey.)

journeys.note_ko: 로마에서 풀려난 뒤 서바나(스페인)와 동방(그레데·에베소·마게도냐)을 다시 돌며 교회를 굳히고, 디모데·디도에게 목회서신을 쓴다. 끝내 다시 갇혀 순교한다. (목회서신과 전승으로 재구성한 마지막 여정.)
journeys.note_en: Released from Rome, Paul revisits the west (Spain) and the east (Crete, Ephesus, Macedonia), strengthening churches and writing the Pastoral Letters to Timothy and Titus — until a final arrest and martyrdom. (A last journey reconstructed from the Pastoral Letters and tradition.)
